senses.gloss: to dream (something) or about [with بِ (bi)] (something) while asleep
to dream (something) or about [with بِ (bi)] (something) while asleep; to muse, reflect, or meditate on [with بِ (bi)]; to vision, to image
to experience the first seminal ejaculation; to mature physically or sexually; to attain puberty
to experience the first seminal ejaculation; to become a legal adult or to be perceived as such
to mature mentally or to be perceived as such; to act or think like an adult
to be gentle, forgiving, merciful, or lenient; to be mild-mannered

to restrain oneself or to be restrained or checked (especially emotionally and psychologically); to forbear, to refrain, to abstain, to withhold; to be long-suffering, to be patient or enduring; to mellow
verbal noun of حَلَمَ (ḥalama) (form I): dreaming
a dream (something experienced during sleep); vision
a wish, a desire, an aspiration; a daydream (something fantasised about or desired)

examples.text: وَإِذَا بَلَغَ ٱلْأَطْفَالُ مِنْكُمُ ٱلْحُلُمَ فَلْيَسْتَأْذِنُوا كَمَا ٱسْتَأْذَنَ ٱلَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِهِمْ
examples.english: And if the children among you reach puberty, they shall ask for permission as those who did before them.

senses.gloss: mental, psychological, or comportmental maturity; prudence; rationality; discretion

examples.english: You are more patient than me.

examples.text: لَا يَغُرَّنَّكَ حِلْمِي فَصَبْرِي بَدَأَ يَنْفَدُ.
examples.english: Let not my forbearance deceive you, for my patience is running out.

etymology.text: Akin to Aramaic חֶלְמָא (ḥelmā), Classical Syriac ܚܠܡܐ (ḥelmā), Hebrew חֲלוֹם (ḥalóm), Amharic ሕልም (ḥəlm), Ugaritic 𐎈𐎍𐎎 (ḥlm). For the significations of the root in Arabic, compare م ن ي (m n y), whence أُمْنِيَة (ʔumniya, “a wish”) and مَنِيّ (maniyy, “a fluid considered conceptive; semen”).
